# multipart

```go
import "mime/multipart"
```

Package multipart implements MIME multipart parsing, as defined in RFC 2046.

The implementation is sufficient for HTTP \(RFC 2388\) and the multipart bodies generated by popular browsers.

### Limits

To protect against malicious inputs, this package sets limits on the size of the MIME data it processes.

[Reader.NextPart](<#Reader.NextPart>) and [Reader.NextRawPart](<#Reader.NextRawPart>) limit the number of headers in a part to 10000 and [Reader.ReadForm](<#Reader.ReadForm>) limits the total number of headers in all FileHeaders to 10000. These limits may be adjusted with the GODEBUG=multipartmaxheaders=\<values\> setting.

Reader.ReadForm further limits the number of parts in a form to 1000. This limit may be adjusted with the GODEBUG=multipartmaxparts=\<value\> setting.

## Variables

<a name="ErrMessageTooLarge"></a>ErrMessageTooLarge is returned by ReadForm if the message form data is too large to be processed.

```go
var ErrMessageTooLarge = errors.New("multipart: message too large")
```

```go
func FileContentDisposition(fieldname, filename string) string
```

<a name="File"></a>

## type [File](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/formdata.go#L287-L292>)

File is an interface to access the file part of a multipart message. Its contents may be either stored in memory or on disk. If stored on disk, the File's underlying concrete type will be an \*os.File.

```go
type File interface {
    io.Reader
    io.ReaderAt
    io.Seeker
    io.Closer
}
```

<a name="FileHeader"></a>

## type [FileHeader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/formdata.go#L256-L265>)

A FileHeader describes a file part of a multipart request.

```go
type FileHeader struct {
    Filename string
    Header   textproto.MIMEHeader
    Size     int64
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FileHeader.Open"></a>

### func \(\*FileHeader\) [Open](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/formdata.go#L268>)

```go
func (fh *FileHeader) Open() (File, error)
```

<a name="Form"></a>

## type [Form](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/formdata.go#L234-L237>)

Form is a parsed multipart form. Its File parts are stored either in memory or on disk, and are accessible via the [\\\*FileHeader](<#FileHeader>)'s Open method. Its Value parts are stored as strings. Both are keyed by field name.

```go
type Form struct {
    Value map[string][]string
    File  map[string][]*FileHeader
}
```

```go
func (f *Form) RemoveAll() error
```

RemoveAll removes any temporary files associated with a [Form](<#Form>).

<a name="Part"></a>

## type [Part](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/multipart.go#L52-L72>)

A Part represents a single part in a multipart body.

```go
type Part struct {
    // The headers of the body, if any, with the keys canonicalized
    // in the same fashion that the Go http.Request headers are.
    // For example, "foo-bar" changes case to "Foo-Bar"
    Header textproto.MIMEHeader
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```


<a name="Part.FileName"></a>

### func \(\*Part\) [FileName](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/multipart.go#L91>)

```go
func (p *Part) FileName() string
```

FileName returns the filename parameter of the [Part](<#Part>)'s Content\-Disposition header. If not empty, the filename is passed through filepath.Base \(which is platform dependent\) before being returned.

<a name="Part.FormName"></a>

### func \(\*Part\) [FormName](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/multipart.go#L76>)

```go
func (p *Part) FormName() string
```

FormName returns the name parameter if p has a Content\-Disposition of type "form\-data". Otherwise it returns the empty string.

<a name="Part.Read"></a>

```go
func (p *Part) Read(d []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Read reads the body of a part, after its headers and before the next part \(if any\) begins.

<a name="Reader"></a>

## type [Reader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/multipart.go#L333-L344>)

Reader is an iterator over parts in a MIME multipart body. Reader's underlying parser consumes its input as needed. Seeking isn't supported.

```go
type Reader struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewReader"></a>

### func [NewReader](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/multipart.go#L119>)

```go
func NewReader(r io.Reader, boundary string) *Reader
```

NewReader creates a new multipart [Reader](<#Reader>) reading from r using the given MIME boundary.

The boundary is usually obtained from the "boundary" parameter of the message's "Content\-Type" header. Use [mime.ParseMediaType](<https://pkg.go.dev/mime/#ParseMediaType>) to parse such headers.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"mime"
	"mime/multipart"
	"net/mail"
	"strings"
)

func main() {
	msg := &mail.Message{
		Header: map[string][]string{
			"Content-Type": {"multipart/mixed; boundary=foo"},
		},
		Body: strings.NewReader(
			"--foo\r\nFoo: one\r\n\r\nA section\r\n" +
				"--foo\r\nFoo: two\r\n\r\nAnd another\r\n" +
				"--foo--\r\n"),
	}
	mediaType, params, err := mime.ParseMediaType(msg.Header.Get("Content-Type"))
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if strings.HasPrefix(mediaType, "multipart/") {
		mr := multipart.NewReader(msg.Body, params["boundary"])
		for {
			p, err := mr.NextPart()
			if err == io.EOF {
				return
			}
			if err != nil {
				log.Fatal(err)
			}
			slurp, err := io.ReadAll(p)
			if err != nil {
				log.Fatal(err)
			}
			fmt.Printf("Part %q: %q\n", p.Header.Get("Foo"), slurp)
		}
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
Part "one": "A section"
Part "two": "And another"
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Reader.NextPart"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [NextPart](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/multipart.go#L371>)

```go
func (r *Reader) NextPart() (*Part, error)
```

NextPart returns the next part in the multipart or an error. When there are no more parts, the error [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>) is returned.

As a special case, if the "Content\-Transfer\-Encoding" header has a value of "quoted\-printable", that header is instead hidden and the body is transparently decoded during Read calls.

<a name="Reader.NextRawPart"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [NextRawPart](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/multipart.go#L380>)

```go
func (r *Reader) NextRawPart() (*Part, error)
```

NextRawPart returns the next part in the multipart or an error. When there are no more parts, the error [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>) is returned.

Unlike [Reader.NextPart](<#Reader.NextPart>), it does not have special handling for "Content\-Transfer\-Encoding: quoted\-printable".

<a name="Reader.ReadForm"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [ReadForm](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/formdata.go#L32>)

```go
func (r *Reader) ReadForm(maxMemory int64) (*Form, error)
```

ReadForm parses an entire multipart message whose parts have a Content\-Disposition of "form\-data". It stores up to maxMemory bytes \+ 10MB \(reserved for non\-file parts\) in memory. File parts which can't be stored in memory will be stored on disk in temporary files. It returns [ErrMessageTooLarge](<#ErrMessageTooLarge>) if all non\-file parts can't be stored in memory.

<a name="Writer.CreateFormField"></a>

### func \(\*Writer\) [CreateFormField](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/writer.go#L157>)

```go
func (w *Writer) CreateFormField(fieldname string) (io.Writer, error)
```

CreateFormField calls [Writer.CreatePart](<#Writer.CreatePart>) with a header using the given field name.

<a name="Writer.CreateFormFile"></a>

### func \(\*Writer\) [CreateFormFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/writer.go#L148>)

```go
func (w *Writer) CreateFormFile(fieldname, filename string) (io.Writer, error)
```

CreateFormFile is a convenience wrapper around [Writer.CreatePart](<#Writer.CreatePart>). It creates a new form\-data header with the provided field name and file name.

<a name="Writer.CreatePart"></a>

### func \(\*Writer\) [CreatePart](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/writer.go#L98>)

```go
func (w *Writer) CreatePart(header textproto.MIMEHeader) (io.Writer, error)
```

CreatePart creates a new multipart section with the provided header. The body of the part should be written to the returned [Writer](<#Writer>). After calling CreatePart, any previous part may no longer be written to.

<a name="Writer.FormDataContentType"></a>

### func \(\*Writer\) [FormDataContentType](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/writer.go#L75>)

```go
func (w *Writer) FormDataContentType() string
```

FormDataContentType returns the Content\-Type for an HTTP multipart/form\-data with this [Writer](<#Writer>)'s Boundary.

<a name="Writer.SetBoundary"></a>

### func \(\*Writer\) [SetBoundary](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/writer.go#L46>)

```go
func (w *Writer) SetBoundary(boundary string) error
```

SetBoundary overrides the [Writer](<#Writer>)'s default randomly\-generated boundary separator with an explicit value.

SetBoundary must be called before any parts are created, may only contain certain ASCII characters, and must be non\-empty and at most 70 bytes long.

<a name="Writer.WriteField"></a>

### func \(\*Writer\) [WriteField](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/mime/multipart/writer.go#L172>)

```go
func (w *Writer) WriteField(fieldname, value string) error
```

WriteField calls [Writer.CreateFormField](<#Writer.CreateFormField>) and then writes the given value.
